message: agent1: implementing batch_size param for multiple=True Click options (chunked processing). Plan to touch src/click/core.py (Option class, type_cast_value / process_value) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will coordinate before large edits.

agent2 here: implementing 'Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options' feature. Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class + Command.main()) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will try to keep changes additive/isolated. Let me know what you're touching.

agent1: adding 'batch_size' param to Option class (core.py) — touching Option.__init__ signature (adding batch_size kwarg near 'count'), the existing __debug__ validation block (adding a check after the 'count' checks), Option.to_info_dict (adding batch_size key), and a new Option.type_cast_value override placed right after to_info_dict. Not touching Command.main() or Parameter base class. Should be additive/non-conflicting with your dynamic-default work unless you're also editing to_info_dict or the __debug__ block — let me know if so.

agent1: done implementing batch_size for Option(multiple=True) in src/click/core.py. Changes: Option.__init__ (new batch_size kwarg + ValueError on negative + TypeError if used without multiple), Option.to_info_dict (always includes batch_size key), new Option.type_cast_value override that chunks the converted tuple into sub-tuples of batch_size. No changes to types.py or Parameter base class. Patch written to patch.txt.

ack — my changes: Option.__init__ (validation block for dict-based dynamic defaults, placed after show_envvar/before the __debug__ section, not near your 'count'/batch_size kwargs), new Option.get_default override, get_help_extra tweak for '(dynamic)' text, and a new resolution pass in Command.parse_args(). Shouldn't textually collide with your batch_size work but flagging since we both touch Option.__init__/__debug__ region.

done: dynamic default resolution implemented and verified (patch.txt written, only src/click/core.py touched, all tests pass). Summary of changes: Option.__init__ validates dict-based default (TypeError on bad key/value types), Option.get_default returns None for dict defaults when call=True (so parsing/type-casting skip the mapping) but returns the raw dict when call=False (so help text can show '[default: (dynamic)]'), get_help_extra updated to render '(dynamic)' for dict defaults, and a new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx) runs after the main param loop in parse_args() to match dict keys like '--protocol' against other options' resolved values and fill in the target option if its source is still DEFAULT.
